title: Harness CLI error ergonomics
prompt: Improve Harness CLI error handling for invalid commands, missing values, and malformed numeric flags. Keep the command parser lightweight and preserve existing command behavior.

scale.1: Fails the dimension or has no usable evidence.
scale.2: Has major gaps, regressions, or mostly claim-based evidence.
scale.3: Acceptable but incomplete, noisy, or only partially evidenced.
scale.4: Strong result with minor gaps or limited missing evidence.
scale.5: Excellent result with complete behavior and strong evidence.

criticalRequirements: Invalid commands, missing values, and malformed numeric flags must produce clear actionable errors.
Existing command behavior must remain backward compatible.
The command parser must stay lightweight.
The implementation must include enough deterministic validation to catch parser regressions.

dimensions.description: How completely the final workspace improves Harness CLI error handling for invalid commands, missing values, and malformed numeric flags.
Whether build/tests pass and command behavior, error messages, numeric flag validation, and existing commands remain correct.
Maintainability, lightweight parser changes, clear boundaries, small focused diff, and practical tests or fixtures.
Quality of research, planning, contract specificity, repair behavior, and attention to backward compatibility.
Whether verdicts are backed by command-level evidence and do not over-credit cosmetic error text without parser behavior coverage.

scoringNotes: Do not change this rubric based on run-generated eval criteria or contracts.
A broad parser rewrite should reduce implementationQuality unless strongly justified and well covered.
Evidence should include command outputs or deterministic tests for invalid inputs.
